files.patch:
@@ -405,1 +405,1 @@ - while (idom != entry) { + while (idom != null) { @@ -422,0 +422,2 @@ + *

+ * The entry block has no immediate dominator, since it is not strictly dominated by any other block. @@ -424,0 +426,1 @@ + * @see Body#immediateDominators() @@ -426,6 +429,1 @@ - if (this == ancestorBody().entryBlock()) { - return null; - } - - Map idoms = ancestorBody().immediateDominators(); - return idoms.get(this); + return ancestorBody().immediateDominators().get(this); @@ -437,3 +435,7 @@ - * If this block has no successors then this method returns the synthetic block - * {@link Body#IPDOM_EXIT} representing the synthetic exit used to compute - * the immediate post dominators. + * If this block is one of many block's in the same body with no successors, then there are multiple exit blocks, + * and the immediate post dominator of those blocks is the synthetic block {@link Body#IPDOM_EXIT} representing the + * single exit block. Otherwise, if this block is the only block in the body with no successors, then that block is + * the single exit block, and this method returns {@code null}. + *

+ * Both this block and the immediate post dominator (if defined) have the same parent body, except for the + * synthetic block {@link Body#IPDOM_EXIT}. @@ -441,2 +443,2 @@ - * Both this block and the immediate post dominator (if defined) have the same parent body, - * except for the synthetic block {@link Body#IPDOM_EXIT}. + * The immediate post dominator is the unique block that strictly post dominates this block, but does not strictly + * post dominate any other block that strictly post dominates this block. @@ -444,2 +446,1 @@ - * The immediate post dominator is the unique block that strictly post dominates this block, - * but does not strictly post dominate any other block that strictly post dominates this block. + * The exit block has no immediate post dominator, since it is not strictly post dominated by any other block. @@ -447,1 +448,4 @@ - * @return the immediate post dominator of this block, otherwise {@code Body#IPDOM_EXIT}. + * @return the immediate post dominator of this block, {@code Body#IPDOM_EXIT} if the synthetic exit is this block's + * immediate post dominator, or {@code null} if this block is the single exit block. + * @throws IllegalStateException if there is no single exit block, synthesized or otherwise + * @see Body#immediatePostDominators() @@ -450,3 +454,1 @@ - Map ipdoms = ancestorBody().immediatePostDominators(); - Block ipdom = ipdoms.get(this); - return ipdom == this ? Body.IPDOM_EXIT : ipdom; + return ancestorBody().immediatePostDominators().get(this); @@ -455,1 +457,1 @@ - // @@@ isPostDominatedBy and immediatePostDominator + // @@@ isPostDominatedBy

@@ -147,1 +147,7 @@ - * Returns a map of block to its immediate dominator. + * Returns a map of block to its immediate dominator, for all blocks in this body. + *

+ * A block's immediate dominator is the unique block that strictly dominates that block, but does not strictly + * dominate any other block that strictly dominates that block. + *

+ * The entry block has no immediate dominator, since it is not strictly dominated by any other block. Its + * corresponding entry in the map has a {@code null} value. @@ -150,0 +156,1 @@ + * @see Block#immediateDominator() @@ -169,1 +176,1 @@ - doms.put(entryBlock(), entryBlock()); + doms.put(entryBlock(), null); @@ -187,1 +194,1 @@ - assert b.predecessors().isEmpty() || newIdom != null : b; + assert newIdom != null : b; @@ -272,1 +279,8 @@ - * Returns a map of block to its immediate post dominator. + * Returns a map of block to its immediate post dominator, for all blocks in this body. + *

+ * If there are two or more blocks with no successors then a single exit block is synthesized using the + * {@link #IPDOM_EXIT} block, which represents the immediate post dominator of those blocks. The returned map + * will contain an entry mapping {@code IPDOM_EXIT} to {@code null}. + *

+ * A block's immediate post dominator is the unique block that strictly post dominates that block, but does not + * strictly post dominate any other block that strictly post dominates that block. @@ -274,3 +288,2 @@ - * If there are two or more blocks with no successors then - * a single exit point is synthesized using the {@link #IPDOM_EXIT} - * block, which represents the immediate post dominator of those blocks. + * The exit block has no immediate post dominator, since it is not strictly post dominated by any other block. Its + * corresponding entry in the map has a {@code null} value. @@ -279,0 +292,2 @@ + * @throws IllegalStateException if there is no single exit block, synthesized or otherwise + * @see Block#immediatePostDominator() @@ -288,6 +303,5 @@ - if (nSuccessors) { - pdoms.put(IPDOM_EXIT, IPDOM_EXIT); - } else { - Block exit = blocks.getLast(); - assert blocks.stream().filter(b -> b.successors().isEmpty()).findFirst().orElseThrow() == exit; - pdoms.put(exit, exit); + List exits = blocks.stream().filter(b -> b.successors().isEmpty()).toList(); + switch (exits.size()) { + case 0 -> throw new IllegalStateException(); + case 1 -> pdoms.put(exits.getFirst(), null); + default -> pdoms.put(IPDOM_EXIT, null); @@ -624,2 +638,3 @@ - * @throws IllegalStateException if any connected body builder is not successfully finished - * @throws IllegalStateException if any connected body builder built and its parent operation is unplaced + * @throws IllegalStateException if any connected body builder finishes unsuccessfully + * @throws IllegalStateException if any connected body builder finishes successfully and its body's parent + * operation is unplaced
